headline: Kelantan and Terengganu Receive Lowest Per Capita Federal Allocation
context: Budget 2026 analysis revealed that Kelantan and Terengganu received the lowest per capita federal development allocation at RM680 and RM720 respectively, compared to the national average of RM1,450, raising allegations of political punishment.

cards.big: Kelantan gets RM680 per capita in federal development funds — less than half the RM1,450 national average
cards.sub: Both PAS-governed states consistently receive lowest allocations regardless of which coalition holds Putrajaya

cards.big: Kelantan's GDP per capita at RM15,400 is 58% below national average — federal underinvestment perpetuates the gap
cards.sub: Infrastructure spending per km² in Kelantan is RM42,000 versus RM280,000 in Selangor — a 6.7x disparity

cards.big: Kelantan's poverty rate at 12.4% is 3.6x the national average — children in the state face the poorest educational outcomes
cards.sub: 88 schools in Kelantan classified as dilapidated versus only 12 in Selangor despite similar student populations

cards.big: Federal-state fiscal transfers have no constitutional formula — allocations are discretionary cabinet decisions each budget cycle
cards.sub: Australia and Germany use legislated equalisation formulas — Malaysia's system enables political reward and punishment

cards.big: Punishing Kelantan's voters for their political choices by withholding development funds punishes children who never voted
Should Malaysia adopt a needs-based fiscal equalisation formula that guarantees minimum per capita allocation regardless of state politics?
